mission.title: caddy: Frequent hangs when using http/2 push
mission.description: Frequent hangs when using http/2 push. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify caddy troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your caddy installation:
```bash
caddy version
caddy validate --config /etc/caddy/Caddyfile
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review caddy configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant caddy configuration:
My team and I use Caddy as a reverse proxy, and we rely on HTTP/2 Push a lot. We've started with v2.2.1, on certain configurations we experienced random hangs.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Frequent hangs when using http/2 push
steps.description:
This issue was caused by a Go standard library bug (golang/go#42534) affecting HTTP/2 server push. Chrome has since dropped HTTP/2 push support entirely.

To resolve the hangs, remove any `push` directives from your Caddyfile:
```
# Before (causes hangs):
example.com {
  push
  reverse_proxy backend:8080
}

# After (remove push directive):
example.com {
  reverse_proxy backend:8080
}
```
HTTP/2 push is no longer supported by major browsers and should not be used.

steps.title: Confirm Frequent hangs when using http/2 push is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test caddy to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: The root cause of the HTTP/2 push hangs was traced to a Go standard library bug (golang/go#42534) in the HTTP/2 server push implementation. The issue was ultimately closed as won't-fix because Chrome dropped support for HTTP/2 server push entirely, making the feature obsolete. Users experiencing this issue should remove HTTP/2 push directives from their Caddy configuration.

prerequisites.description: A working caddy installation or development environment.
